1: // Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.
2: // SPDX-License-Identifier: MPL-2.0
3: 
4: package tcp
5: 
6: import (
7: 	"context"
8: 	"io"
9: 	"net"
10: 	"sync"
11: 
12: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/daemon/worker/proxy"
13: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/errors"
14: 	"google.golang.org/protobuf/types/known/anypb"
15: )
16: 
17: func init() {
18: 	err := proxy.RegisterHandler(proxy.TcpHandlerName, handleProxy)
19: 	if err != nil {
20: 		panic(err)
21: 	}
22: }
23: 
24: // handleProxy creates a tcp proxy between the incoming conn and the
25: // connection created by the ProxyDialer.
26: //
27: // handleProxy returns a ProxyConnFn which starts the copy between the
28: // connections and blocks until an error (EOF on happy path) is received on
29: // either connection.
30: func handleProxy(ctx context.Context, _ proxy.DecryptFn, conn net.Conn, out *proxy.ProxyDialer, connId string, _ *anypb.Any, _ proxy.RecordingManager) (proxy.ProxyConnFn, error) {
31: 	const op = "tcp.HandleProxy"
32: 	switch {
33: 	case conn == nil:
34: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "conn is nil")
35: 	case out == nil:
36: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "proxy dialer is nil")
37: 	case len(connId) == 0:
38: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "connection id is empty")
39: 	}
40: 	remoteConn, err := out.Dial(ctx)
41: 	if err != nil {
42: 		return nil, err
43: 	}
44: 
45: 	return func(ctx context.Context) {
46: 		connWg := new(sync.WaitGroup)
47: 		connWg.Add(2)
48: 		go func() {
49: 			defer connWg.Done()
50: 			_, _ = io.Copy(conn, remoteConn)
51: 			_ = conn.Close()
52: 			_ = remoteConn.Close()
53: 		}()
54: 		go func() {
55: 			defer connWg.Done()
56: 			_, _ = io.Copy(remoteConn, conn)
57: 			_ = remoteConn.Close()
58: 			_ = conn.Close()
59: 		}()
60: 		connWg.Wait()
61: 	}, nil
62: }

(End of file - total 62 lines)

103: func TestHandleTcpProxyV1(t *testing.T) {
104: 	t.Parallel()
105: 	require, assert := require.New(t), assert.New(t)
106: 
107: 	ctx, cancelCtx := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
108: 	clientConn, proxyConn := proxy.TestWsConn(t, ctx)
109: 	require.NotNil(clientConn)
110: 	require.NotNil(proxyConn)
111: 
112: 	l, err := net.Listen("tcp", "127.0.0.1:0")
113: 	require.NoError(err)
114: 	defer l.Close()
115: 
116: 	var endpointConn net.Conn
117: 	var endpointErr error
118: 	var wg sync.WaitGroup
119: 	wg.Add(1)
120: 	go func() {
121: 		endpointConn, endpointErr = l.Accept()
122: 		require.NoError(endpointErr)
123: 		defer endpointConn.Close()
124: 
125: 		wg.Done()
126: 		// block waiting for test to complete
127: 		<-ctx.Done()
128: 	}()
129: 
130: 	// Create mock data for session management section of HandleTcpProxyV1
131: 	sessClient := pbs.NewMockSessionServiceClient()
132: 	sessClient.LookupSessionFn = func(_ context.Context, request *pbs.LookupSessionRequest) (*pbs.LookupSessionResponse, error) {
133: 		cert, _, _ := createTestCert(t)
134: 		return &pbs.LookupSessionResponse{
135: 			Authorization: &targets.SessionAuthorizationData{
136: 				SessionId:   request.GetSessionId(),
137: 				Certificate: cert,
138: 			},
139: 			Expiration: timestamppb.New(time.Now().Add(time.Hour)),
140: 		}, nil
141: 	}
142: 	sessClient.AuthorizeConnectionFn = func(_ context.Context, req *pbs.AuthorizeConnectionRequest) (*pbs.AuthorizeConnectionResponse, error) {
143: 		return &pbs.AuthorizeConnectionResponse{
144: 			ConnectionId:    "mock-connection",
145: 			Status:          pbs.CONNECTIONSTATUS_CONNECTIONSTATUS_AUTHORIZED,
146: 			ConnectionsLeft: -1,
147: 		}, nil
148: 	}
149: 	sessClient.ConnectConnectionFn = func(_ context.Context, _ *pbs.ConnectConnectionRequest) (*pbs.ConnectConnectionResponse, error) {
150: 		return &pbs.ConnectConnectionResponse{
151: 			Status: pbs.CONNECTIONSTATUS_CONNECTIONSTATUS_CONNECTED,
152: 		}, nil
153: 	}
154: 	manager, err := session.NewManager(sessClient)
155: 	require.NoError(err)
156: 	s, err := manager.LoadLocalSession(ctx, "one", "workerid")
157: 	require.NoError(err)
158: 	_, connCancelFn := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
159: 	resp, _, err := s.RequestAuthorizeConnection(ctx, "workerid", connCancelFn)
160: 	require.NoError(err)
161: 
162: 	tDial, err := proxy.NewProxyDialer(ctx, func(...proxy.Option) (net.Conn, error) {
163: 		return net.Dial("tcp", l.Addr().String())
164: 	})
165: 	require.NoError(err)
166: 	conn := websocket.NetConn(ctx, proxyConn, websocket.MessageBinary)
167: 
168: 	go func() {
169: 		fn, err := handleProxy(ctx, nil, conn, tDial, resp.GetConnectionId(), resp.GetProtocolContext(), nil)
170: 		t.Cleanup(func() {
171: 			// Use of the t.Cleanup is so we can check the state of the returned
172: 			// error since it isn't valid to call `t.FailNow()` from a goroutine.
173: 			// https://pkg.go.dev/testing#T.FailNow
174: 			require.NoError(err)
175: 		})
176: 		fn(context.Background())
177: 	}()
178: 
179: 	// wait for HandleTcpProxyV1 to dial endpoint
180: 	wg.Wait()
181: 	netConn := websocket.NetConn(ctx, clientConn, websocket.MessageBinary)
182: 
183: 	// Write from endpoint to client
184: 	writeLen, err := endpointConn.Write([]byte("endpoint write to client via proxy"))
185: 	require.NoError(err)
186: 
187: 	b := make([]byte, writeLen)
188: 	readLen, err := netConn.Read(b)
189: 	require.NoError(err)
190: 	assert.Equal(writeLen, readLen)
191: 	assert.Equal("endpoint write to client via proxy", string(b))
192: 
193: 	// Write from client to endpoint
194: 	writeLen, err = netConn.Write([]byte("client write to endpoint via proxy"))
195: 	require.NoError(err)
196: 
197: 	b1 := make([]byte, writeLen)
198: 	readLen, err = endpointConn.Read(b1)
199: 	require.NoError(err)
